# package(name:url:_:)

**Type Method**

Adds a package dependency starting with a specific minimum version, going up to and including a specific maximum version.

## Declaration

```swift
static func package(name: String, url: String, _ range: ClosedRange<Version>) -> Package.Dependency
```

## Parameters

- **name**: The name of the package, or `nil` to deduce it from the URL.
- **url**: The valid Git URL of the package.
- **range**: The closed version range requirement.

## Return Value

A `Package.Dependency` instance.

## Discussion

The following example allows the Swift Package Manager to pick versions 1.2.3, 1.2.4, 1.2.5, as well as 1.2.6.

```swift
.package(url: "https://example.com/example-package.git", "1.2.3"..."1.2.6"),
```

The following example allows the Swift Package Manager to pick versions between 1.0.0 and 2.0.0

```swift
.package(url: "https://example.com/example-package.git", .upToNextMajor(from: "1.0.0"),
```

The following example allows the Swift Package Manager to pick versions between 1.0.0 and 1.1.0

```swift
.package(url: "https://example.com/example-package.git", .upToNextMinor(from: "1.0.0"),
```

## Deprecated methods

- **package(name:url:_:)**: Adds a remote package dependency with a given version requirement.
- **package(name:url:_:)**: Adds a package dependency starting with a specific minimum version, up to but not including a specified maximum version.
- **package(name:url:branch:)**: Adds a remote package dependency given a branch requirement.
- **package(name:url:from:)**: Adds a package dependency that uses the version requirement, starting with the given minimum version, going up to the next major version.
- **package(name:url:revision:)**: Adds a remote package dependency given a revision requirement.
- **package(url:_:)**: Adds a remote package dependency given a version requirement.
- **name**: The name of the dependency.
- **url**: The Git URL of the package dependency.
